lines.text: Tucker here, East Side perimeter, near the Schenk's Corners commercial strip. We're {{ELAPSED}} in and the numbers are climbing faster than the models predicted.
Madison-wide count is at 61 kills as of {{CLOCK}}. That's 23 for this hour alone — up 31% from the same window last year. East Side is pulling most of that weight, running about 34% above trend.

I'm seeing increased foot traffic moving northwest toward the Capitol corridor. Organized movement, not random. Weapons manifest is heavy on bladed — saw three separate groups carrying machetes and crowbars in the last twelve minutes.
There's... there's some interference on my end. Signal's been cutting in and out for the last few minutes. Hold on, I'm repositioning the antenna — my rig's old and tonight it's really showing it.

Back. Okay. Neighborhood watch presence on Atwood is minimal. I count maybe four armed civilians on the west side of the overpass, and they're... they're holding position but not advancing. Smart. Stay contained.

Weather's degrading. Wind's picked up to about 15 miles per hour, gusting higher. Caroline was right about the system moving in early. Visibility's dropping and — something just moved across the street about 40 meters north of my position, I'm going to [CUT OFF]

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide: 61 cumulative (23 this hour). East Side +34% trend. Capitol Square activity increasing.
Atwood Ave commercial: minimal watch presence. Movement trending northwest toward Capitol perimeter.
Cold front arriving earlier than forecast. Visibility down to 4 miles. Wind gusts to 22 mph expected within the hour.
Field equipment experiencing signal degradation. Multiple zones reporting interference tonight — possible jammer activity or atmospheric conditions.
